Electrical Signature Analysis

The future of condition monitoring
is electrical signal analysis.

ESA reads the motor's electrical signal to detect faults across the full drivetrain: motor, coupling, gearbox, and driven load.

Without installing sensors on the asset.

Monitoring 20,000+ industrial assets across 5 continents.

Why electrical signals? Faults ESA detects

Why faults show up in the electrical signal

Every drivetrain component affects the motor's electrical signal. Changes in bearings, alignment, cavitation, or load alter the current pattern the motor draws.

Electrical Signature Analysis measures these patterns to detect faults early, typically weeks to months before failure.

Traditional vibration monitoring requires sensors installed on the machine. ESA measures at the Motor Control Center instead. No sensor touches the asset.

  • No sensors on the asset: Measure at the MCC, not the machine
  • Physics-based detection: Motor current patterns reveal drivetrain condition
  • Fleet-wide coverage: Monitor 100+ motors from a single installation

Validated across 20,000+ monitored industrial machines worldwide.

Faults ESA detects

ESA detects mechanical and process faults across the drivetrain, directly from the motor's electrical signal.

Faults detected weeks to months before failure across 20,000+ monitored machines.

Bearing Wear

Spalling and friction in rolling element bearings altering motor load patterns

Misalignment

Angular or parallel misalignment increasing mechanical load and torque oscillations

Unbalance

Rotor mass imbalance producing periodic load variation in the electrical signal

Belt Degradation

Slippage and wear altering rotational load patterns in belt-driven systems

Cavitation

Vapor bubble collapse in pumps causing fluctuating hydraulic load

Blockage

Flow restrictions increasing pump load and energy consumption

How ESA compares

Traditional monitoring instruments individual machines. ESA monitors the entire fleet from the electrical system.

Traditional Vibration MCSA SAM4 ESA
Sensor on the asset Required Not required Not required
Installation effort Days per asset Hours per asset One MCC installation
Asset coverage Critical assets only Limited Entire motor fleet
Fault detection scope Mechanical faults Electrical faults Mechanical + electrical + process
Diagnostics Manual analysis Manual analysis AI-assisted diagnostics
Typical cost per asset $10K+ installed $2-5K installed $200-500
